headings.text: Cosmetic & Restorative Dentistry in Bellevue WA
Cosmetic & Restorative Dentistry Options in Bellevue WA
Restorative Dentistry Options at AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry

paragraphs: For most people, the goal of oral hygiene is strictly health. Keeping your teeth and gums in good shape is always important, but it’s not the only thing to consider. More and more people are visiting cosmetic dentists, in an effort to make their smiles as beautiful as possible.

Cosmetic dentistry in Bellevue WA is exactly what it sounds like. This is a specialized branch of dentistry in which cosmetic dentists have focused on optional procedures that dramatically improve the appearance of a person’s mouth. For anyone that feels there’s something off about their teeth, or if you need to maintain a perfect smile for your profession, cosmetic dentistry may be the answer.

When you see people with flawless teeth, chances are they weren’t born like that and had some cosmetic dentistry done to give their mouth that perfect finish. If you’re interested in getting your mouth to look its best, here are the ways that cosmetic dentistry can help.

If you're wondering how people get those flawless smiles, the secret often lies in cosmetic dentistry. Many of the patients we see at AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry come to us to improve the aesthetics of their teeth. Here are some popular cosmetic procedures available at our clinic:

While off-the-shelf products can whiten teeth, the results are never as brilliant or as long-lasting as a personalized treatment from a professional cosmetic dentist. This is a simple and popular technique that can often be done in one dental visit. For people that need to make a good impression, this is an easy fix to help achieve that.

Dentistry doesn’t have to be just about fixing painful problems. It can also be about helping people to look and feel better about themselves. A smile is one of the most important ways we interact with people. Cosmetic dentistry can bring out the best in your own.
CLICK HERE to learn more about teeth whitening.

Veneers are a shell of a porcelain-like material that is affixed to the teeth. They are more expensive and comprehensive than composite bonding but fulfill the same function. Veneers work to fill in chips, providing more even teeth or filling in awkward gaps and spaces.

This method may require a bit more work on the teeth, possibly even chipping away more of the tooth to ensure a better fit when the veneer is bonded on. However, once the work is done, a veneer is a very durable, aesthetically pleasing technique that often requires no further touch-ups.
CLICK HERE to learn more about veneers.

Invisalign is a company that provides a new alternative to traditional dental braces. You apply this transparent aligner to your teeth that fulfill the same function as traditional braces. Invisalign braces use gentle, constant pressure to your teeth to gradually move them into better alignment so that people can eventually enjoy straighter, evenly spaced teeth.
CLICK HERE for more information about Invisalign.

This is a process through which a plastic or resin is attached to your teeth. This can be used to cover and smooth over chips in your tooth, fill in gaps or awkward spacing, or seal cracks that may appear on certain teeth. Compared to other processes, composite bonding is relatively fast and not as expensive. However, this also means that the work may not last as long as other methods and might require replacement or touching up.

Composite bonding also stains more quickly than other cosmetic dental work. If you smoke, drink a lot of tea or coffee, or even eat a lot of candy; this can affect the appearance and durability of the bond.
When it comes to restorative dentistry, the goal is to bring your teeth back to full functionality while maintaining a natural appearance. Here are some of the services we offer:

Dentures act as a replacement for entire sets of teeth on the upper and lower gums, but dental implants are a more specific technique that replaces an individual tooth. If you have lost only a few teeth due to dental work, or mishap, a dental implant is an excellent substitute.

It should be noted that dental implants, while a durable solution, may also require some extra care. A dental implant essentially affixes a titanium screw into the gums and bone, to which the implant itself is attached. One of the properties of titanium, aside from being an incredibly tough metal, is that it does not provoke a response from the immune system. Extra care should be taken to ensure the gums around the implant are kept clean. If bacteria get into the sensitive gum area around the screw, it could lead to infection.
CLICK HERE to learn more about dental implants.

A dental crown is like a “cap” that is fixed to a single tooth. Dental crowns can be the solution to many issues. These include weakened teeth due to cavities, chipped teeth, or discoloration from illness or infection. The procedure usually involves cementing the crown into place, making it a permanent fixture that shouldn’t require any additional maintenance aside from good oral hygiene.
CLICK HERE for more information about dental crowns.
Today, there are many alternatives to ensure that a missing tooth doesn’t have to become an embarrassment. One technique called a Dental Bridge is a unique solution to the problem.

A dental bridge is designed to address a particular kind of missing tooth problem for patients in Bellevue WA. If there is a “space” in your mouth where you have a tooth, followed by a missing tooth and then another tooth, a dental bridge is a perfect solution.
CLICK HERE for more information about dental bridges.
Dentures or “false teeth” as they are more commonly known have been a reliable oral tool for centuries.

Regular dentures are a full replacement of both sets of teeth, both upper and lower. Partial dentures are any number of configurations for people that may still have some functioning teeth remaining that they wish to keep. This includes some back teeth, front teeth, or any other combination.
CLICK HERE for commonly asked questions about dentures.

Some people may have either a “gummy” or a “toothy” smile. All this means is that the ratio of how much gum appears versus teeth may be off-balance compared to what most people are used to seeing. A “gum lift” is a surgical technique originally used strictly for gum health reasons to reshape gum tissue or the bone underneath on which the gum was fixed.

Today, a gum lift is used to sculpt the way the gums appear in the mouth. This creates proportions that are more normal and evens out any irregularities in the gum distribution for a more symmetrical smile.
CLICK HERE for more information about gum lifts.

Tooth-colored fillings are dental fillings that restore and mimic the natural appearance of tooth structure. In addition to restoring teeth that have fractured or decayed, tooth-colored fillings may also be used cosmetically to change the size, color, and shape of teeth.

Cosmetic dentist visits are mostly “optional,” although you may end up visiting one after an injury or accident. You will never need to visit a cosmetic dentist because of cavities or need to get braces. On those occasions, a cosmetic dentist would be the first to direct to you one of their other peers. Cosmetic dentists in Bellevue WA work primarily with people who already have a set of teeth in relatively good health and merely want to enhance the existing appearance of those teeth.

If you’ve ever looked into your own mouth and wished there was some way to make it look better, visiting a cosmetic dentist may be the solution you need. Call our team at AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry today for more information.

AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry
11216 NE 15th St Suite A
Bellevue WA 98004
(425) 454-3221

9:00am - 5:00pm

Tuesday
9:00am - 5:00pm

9:00am - 5:00pm

Thursday
7:00am - 3:30pm
